g.text: IO a — a description of an effectful computation
bind :: IO a ⟶ (a ⟶ IO b) ⟶ IO b

div.p: An IO a value is a description of a side-effectful computation that, when executed, produces a value of type a.
Nothing runs until the runtime executes it (at main).
bind (>>=) sequences two IO actions: run the first, feed its result to the second. The order of bind determines the order of effects.

g.title: Execute ia to get a, pass a to f, execute the resulting IO b action.
The String read from stdin is passed to the greeting action.
The () result of putStrLn is passed (ignored) to the confirmation action.
Side effect: reads a line from stdin.
Pure: parses the string to Int and wraps in IO.
